data.title: AI models should be excluded from CVE allocation to focus on frameworks and applications, as per NVIDIA. Learn more.
data.description: Discover why according to insights from NVIDIA, it is important for Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) to shift its focus towards frameworks and applications, instead of AI models. By prioritizing frameworks and applications, CVE can address potential vulnerabilities more effectively and prevent security threats. This approach is crucial in ensuring the security of systems and networks. Engaging with this perspective could lead to a more robust security infrastructure and protection against cyber threats.

data.title: Sinclair lifts boycott on Jimmy Kimmel, show to air on ABC stations as before.
data.description: A recent report indicates a significant rise in global temperatures, with an average increase of 1.5 degrees Celsius over the past decade. This spike is notably higher than initially predicted by climate scientists. The rise in temperatures has led to a surge in extreme weather events, including hurricanes, droughts, and wildfires. The report also highlights that carbon dioxide levels have reached a record high of 415 parts per million, contributing to the escalating climate crisis. In response to these alarming trends, experts are calling for immediate and drastic measures to curb greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate the impacts of climate change. The urgency of addressing this crisis is underscored by the fact that we are quickly approaching a point of no return in terms of irreversible damage to the planet.

data.title: Scilex Holding Company closes $150M BTC investment in Datavault AI, receiving 15M shares with potential for 278M total shares at $0.5378 each.
data.description: Scilex Holding Company has invested $150 million in Bitcoin in Datavault AI Inc, acquiring 15,000,000 shares at $0.5378 per share. In total, Scilex is expected to receive up to 278,914,094 shares, with the rest to be issued in a second tranche through a pre-funded warrant. This investment is directed towards non-opioid pain management products for various diseases. The final issuance is subject to approval by Datavault's shareholders to comply with ownership limits.

data.title: AAKP starts 50th annual National Patient Meeting in Dallas, Texas from October 3-5, 2025, the largest kidney patient advocacy event.
data.description: The upcoming National Patient Meeting hosted by the American Association of Kidney Patients (AAKP), the oldest and largest kidney patient advocacy organization in the U.S., will take place from October 3 to October 5, 2025. The event will be held in Dallas, Texas.

data.title: Trump broadens tariffs to include foreign drugs and furniture, bypassing Supreme Court's authority, citing national security laws.
data.description: The tariffs imposed by the president on foreign drugs and furniture are based on national security laws and are not directly addressed by current lawsuits.

data.title: Crypto market rebounds with Bitcoin at $109,654.19, Ethereum at $4,041.31. 141,110 traders liquidated, losing $354.18M.
data.description: The cryptocurrency markets showed signs of recovery following a week of declines, with Bitcoin reaching $110,000 by Friday. Current prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $109,654.19, Ethereum at $4,041.31, Solana at $201.64, XRP at $2.80, Dogecoin at $0.2320, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001195. Coinglass data revealed that 141,110 traders were liquidated in the last 24 hours, totaling $354.18 million in losses. Top gainers in the past day include Plasma (XPL), World Liberty Financial (WLFI), and Story (IP). Additionally, Strategy's MSTR has dropped 7% to $300, prompting discussions on its technical analysis. Vanguard is reportedly considering Crypto ETFs in a potential departure from its previous anti-Bitcoin stance. Peter Schiff has forewarned of a forthcoming 'brutal' market event. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Amplio raises $11.1M Series A led by Hitachi and Yamaha to scale AI surplus solutions for manufacturers.
data.description: Amplio, an AI company revolutionizing surplus management for enterprise manufacturers, revealed its successful completion of a Series A funding round. The round, led by Hitachi Ventures and Yamaha Motor Ventures, raised $11.1 million. Previous supporters, including Construct Capital, Slow Ventures, Alpaca VC, and High Alpha Capital, also contributed to this milestone. The funding will fuel Amplio's plans to enhance its go-to-market strategies and further invest in agentic system development.

data.title: Electronic Arts (EA) to go private in nearly $50 billion deal, stock soars. Investors include Silver Lake and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund. Announcement expected next week. EA known for popular franchises.
data.description: Shares of the video game publisher Electronic Arts, Inc. (NASDAQ: EA) have surged after reports surfaced indicating the company is nearing finalization of a plan to go private in what may become the biggest leveraged buyout to date. The stock price is currently on the rise with the deal valued at a staggering $50 billion. The prospective deal involves investors such as private-equity firm Silver Lake and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund. An official announcement is expected as soon as next week, as reported by the Wall Street Journal. EA is renowned for its popular gaming franchises. For more details, check out the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Foreign investors have poured $1.7 trillion into US markets in the past year with $743.2B in net purchases in 2025 alone. Trump's election led to $1.07T in US asset purchases.
data.description: Foreign investment in U.S. markets has surged during President Donald Trump's return to office, with foreign investors buying a net of $2 billion in U.S. securities in July 2025. This marks the third consecutive month of net inflows, with total purchases of stocks and bonds reaching nearly $1.7 trillion in the past year. Year to date, net foreign purchases hit $743.2 billion, significantly higher than the $214.5 billion in the same period last year and surpassing the $555.4 billion recorded in the first seven months of 2023. Since Trump's election, foreign net purchases total $1.07 trillion. These numbers indicate strong confidence from global investors in America's capital markets and economic stability. The demand for Treasury bonds remains high, reflecting global trust in the U.S. economy.

data.title: Bitcoin could reach over $130,000 by December, according to analyst Benjamin Cowen, as it follows its four-year cycle.
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) is nearing the peak of its four-year cycle according to Benjamin Cowen, founder of IntoTheCryptoverse. Cowen predicts a final surge before the year ends as he observes Bitcoin following its typical post-election-year pattern: reaching a high in August, dipping in September, and breaking out in October. He mentioned that every cycle has historically peaked in Q4 and emphasized that if Bitcoin reaches a price range of $130,000–$140,000 in December, it could signal the top of the cycle. For more details, the complete article is accessible via Benzinga.com.
Company purchases 100,000 of its own shares at €10 each, totaling €1 million.

data.title: Comcast introduces AI to its broadband network, deploying AI-powered network amplifiers for improved reliability, speed, and security.
data.description: Comcast Corp. (NASDAQ: CMCSA) has revealed plans to enhance its broadband infrastructure with artificial intelligence, aiming to establish the nation's most advanced network. By incorporating an extensive array of AI-powered network amplifiers, the company intends to boost reliability, offer multi-gigabit speeds, lower latency, and heighten security. These amplifiers are currently being rolled out across all market sectors and witnessing swift growth. This move showcases Comcast's commitment to innovation and improving customer experience.

data.title: Amgen invests $650 million in US manufacturing amid 100% pharmaceutical tariff announcement by Trump Admin.
data.description: Following the Trump administration's decision to impose a 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals beginning in October, Amgen Inc (NASDAQ: AMGN) revealed plans for a significant $650 million expansion of its U.S. manufacturing network. This expansion is in addition to recent investments by Amgen, such as a $600 million science and innovation center in California, along with manufacturing expansions totaling $900 million in Ohio and $1 billion in North Carolina. The new investment aims to increase drug production capacity at Amgen's biologics manufacturing facility in Juncos and incorporate cutting-edge technologies into their operations. This move comes in light of the escalating trade situation and underscores Amgen's commitment to enhancing its manufacturing capabilities within the United States. More details are available on Benzinga.com.
